description: Invalid NPI — fails Luhn check digit (short-circuits the rule engine)
Invalid NPI — wrong format, not 10 digits (short-circuits the rule engine)
NCCI conflict only — basic metabolic panel billed with comprehensive panel
MUE limit issue only — comprehensive metabolic panel reported above its unit limit

Missing modifier 25 only — problem E/M in a preventive-visit context, no modifier 25. Uses Z00.01 (not Z00.00) so the diagnosis-to-procedure rule, which matches only Z00.00 exactly, does not also fire.

Diagnosis-to-procedure mismatch only — routine exam dx billed with problem E/M. Modifier 25 is present so the modifier rule does not also fire (it only checks modifier absence; the dx-conflict rule ignores modifiers entirely).
Multiple rule-layer findings in one claim — NCCI bundling, MUE overage, dx-procedure mismatch, and missing modifier 25 all at once

Rule-layer-clean preventive visit (CPT/ICD-10 pairing and NCCI/MUE all correct) — but v1.7 live-agent analysis (TD-24) confirmed Medicare Part B does not broadly cover CPT 99395 the way commercial payers do (Medicare preventive visits use HCPCS G-codes instead), so both agents correctly and defensibly flag a real coverage/coding concern. Originally labeled agent-negative; relabeled after live evidence showed this was a golden-set label gap, not agent over-flagging. See GOLD-015 for the deliberately agent-negative baseline.

Problem-oriented E/M with a matching acute diagnosis, dx-procedure pairing is clean — J06.9's own description is unspecified, so the v1.5 ICD-10 rule-layer check still flags it. v1.7 live-agent analysis (TD-24) confirmed the Coding Agent also defensibly questions medical necessity of a CBC for a routine acute URI diagnosis alone — a real coding_defensibility concern, not over-flagging.

Medicare coverage concern — HbA1c billed against a routine-exam diagnosis rather than a diabetes-related one. Coverage Agent finding only; requires --live evaluation (real LLM call) to actually surface this label, since offline mode mocks the Coverage Agent.

Coding defensibility concern — high-level E/M supported only by an unspecified symptom diagnosis. coding_defensibility is an agent-layer finding only; requires --live evaluation to surface, since offline mode mocks the Coding Agent. unspecified_diagnosis is a rule-layer finding (R10.9's own description is unspecified) and surfaces in both modes.
Mixed — rule-layer NCCI conflict alongside a Medicare coverage concern (agent-layer label requires --live evaluation)
Mixed — rule-layer missing modifier 25 alongside a coding defensibility concern (agent-layer label requires --live evaluation)
Kitchen sink — every rule-layer finding plus both agent-layer findings in one claim (agent-layer labels require --live evaluation)

Deliberately agent-negative claim (added v1.7, TD-24) — established-patient office visit for a common, well-documented chronic condition (essential hypertension) with no preventive/coding mismatch, no NCCI/MUE issue, and no plausible coverage or coding-defensibility concern. Verified via a live evaluation probe to confirm Coverage and Coding Agents return no findings before adding to the golden set, addressing the lack of an explicit agent-negative baseline noted in TD-24.
